summary_one_line: A glyph-category sequence forming a virama-terminated USE cluster resolved at end-of-buffer lookahead, reachable by broad byte mutation, not CMP substitution.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input whose bytes, after USE-category classification, drive the Ragel syllable machine into the case 11 transition action (use_virama_terminated_cluster via the te=p;p-- end-of-buffer-resolved arm at line 1013) rather than the broken/standard arms. This requires a sequence of glyph use_category values matching the virama-terminated-cluster grammar with the terminating lookahead, not any specific literal byte constant.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive resolves the branch in 10/10 trials. Its winning seeds carry long homogeneous byte runs (e.g. e7 e7 e7..., dc dc dc..., f5 f5...) and havoc op chains (BytesExpandMutator, BytesRandInsertMutator+WordAddMutator+ByteIncMutator, BytesCopyMutator) that lengthen and vary glyph sequences; broad byte-space exploration eventually produces a category run that the state machine routes into case 11, which fires only at line 1013 with T=7 on the W side.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks the branch in 10/10 trials (T=0 on the L side at line 1013). The blocking arm it does reach is the broken-cluster arm (case 4 / case 17, L-tagged). Its CMP-interception budget is spent generating I2S substitutions keyed to intercepted integer/string compares, but the syllable dispatch is a category-table range lookup (_keys[0] <= use_category() <= _keys[1]), so there is no literal operand for I2SRandReplace to substitute toward the virama-terminated path; that effort displaces the broad havoc exploration naive uses to stumble into the category sequence.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner is naive, whose only mutators are the havoc + token stack with no CMP introspection; the divergence is attributable to plain havoc byte mutation exploring glyph-category sequences, while cmplog's added comparison-substitution stage provides no traction on a table-range dispatch and crowds out that exploration. This is a havoc-only win in the input-to-state-anti direction.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive winning over cmplog with the I2S technique as the differentiating axis.
The blocker is the case 11 transition action calling found_syllable(use_virama_terminated_cluster) with a te=p;p-- end-of-buffer lookahead form.
The blocker arm uses the lookahead form te=p;p-- rather than te=p+1, indicating end-of-buffer resolution.
The syllable state-machine transition is selected by a category-range table lookup, not a literal byte comparison.
naive resolves the branch in all 10 trials while cmplog blocks in all 10 trials.
On the winner side the case 11 arm is taken (T=7) while on the loser side it is never taken (T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1013  T=7 F=40  T=0 F=135  case 11:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The cmplog (loser) seeds reach the broken-cluster arm, which is the loser-blocking path.
naive winning seeds carry havoc mutation ops that lengthen and vary byte/glyph sequences.
naive winning seeds contain long homogeneous byte runs producing repeated glyph categories.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: b4 00 00 ff fe e7 e7 e7 e7 e7 e7 e7 e7 00 00 80

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S mutator; its mutators are only the havoc + token stack with no CMP introspection.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes an intercepted CMP operand at matching input offsets, which presupposes a literal operand to match.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Lineage verification not required: the decisive winner is naive, which has no I2SRandReplace mutator (claimed_mechanism is havoc-only, not I2SRandReplace). The I2S-floor dash-row signal applies to cmplog/vpc winners; here cmplog is the LOSER, so there is no winning cmplog/vpc seed whose chain to inspect for an I2S floor. The win is attributable to naive havoc byte mutation, and cmplog's extra I2S stage failed to help on this category-range table dispatch.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness that gates the case 11 path on a literal multi-byte magic constant comparison (instead of a category-range table lookup) on which cmplog then resolves the branch at least as fast as naive — showing the divergence was a solvable-comparison problem that I2S should have won, contradicting the havoc-only / table-dispatch attribution.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-level link from naive's homogeneous runs to the specific glyph use_category sequence that drives the virama-terminated case 11 arm is inferred from source structure, not directly confirmed by per-glyph category tracing.
